root_text.an8.6:1.1: “Aṭṭhime, bhikkhave, lokadhammā lokaṁ anuparivattanti, loko ca aṭṭha lokadhamme anuparivattati.
root_text.an8.6:1.3: Lābho ca, alābho ca, yaso ca, ayaso ca, nindā ca, pasaṁsā ca, sukhañca, dukkhañca.
root_text.an8.6:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭha lokadhammā lokaṁ anuparivattanti, loko ca ime aṭṭha lokadhamme anuparivattati.
root_text.an8.6:2.1: Assutavato, bhikkhave, puthujjanassa uppajjati lābhopi alābhopi yasopi ayasopi nindāpi pasaṁsāpi sukhampi dukkhampi.
root_text.an8.6:2.2: Sutavatopi, bhikkhave, ariyasāvakassa uppajjati lābhopi alābhopi yasopi ayasopi nindāpi pasaṁsāpi sukhampi dukkhampi.
root_text.an8.6:2.3: Tatra, bhikkhave, ko viseso ko adhippayāso kiṁ nānākaraṇaṁ sutavato ariyasāvakassa assutavatā puthujjanenā”ti?
root_text.an8.6:2.4: “Bhagavaṁmūlakā no, bhante, dhammā bhagavaṁnettikā bhagavaṁpaṭisaraṇā. Sādhu vata, bhante, bhagavantaṁyeva paṭibhātu etassa bhāsitassa attho. Bhagavato sutvā bhikkhū dhāressantī”ti.
root_text.an8.6:3.1: “Tena hi, bhikkhave, suṇātha, sādhukaṁ manasi karotha, bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an8.6:3.2: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an8.6:3.7: so ca kho anicco dukkho vipariṇāmadhammo’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an8.6:3.17: tañca kho aniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhamman’ti yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti.
root_text.an8.6:4.1: Tassa lābhopi cittaṁ pariyādāya tiṭṭhati, alābhopi cittaṁ pariyādāya tiṭṭhati, yasopi cittaṁ pariyādāya tiṭṭhati, ayasopi cittaṁ pariyādāya tiṭṭhati, nindāpi cittaṁ pariyādāya tiṭṭhati, pasaṁsāpi cittaṁ pariyādāya tiṭṭhati, sukhampi cittaṁ pariyādāya tiṭṭhati, dukkhampi cittaṁ pariyādāya tiṭṭhati.
root_text.an8.6:4.2: So uppannaṁ lābhaṁ anurujjhati, alābhe paṭivirujjhati;
root_text.an8.6:4.6: So evaṁ anurodhavirodhasamāpanno na parimuccati jātiyā jarāya maraṇena sokehi paridevehi dukkhehi domanassehi upāyāsehi.
root_text.an8.6:5.1: Sutavato ca kho, bhikkhave, ariyasāvakassa uppajjati lābho.
root_text.an8.6:5.4: so ca kho anicco dukkho vipariṇāmadhammo’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an8.6:5.14: tañca kho aniccaṁ dukkhaṁ vipariṇāmadhamman’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an8.6:6.1: Tassa lābhopi cittaṁ na pariyādāya tiṭṭhati, alābhopi cittaṁ na pariyādāya tiṭṭhati, yasopi cittaṁ na pariyādāya tiṭṭhati, ayasopi cittaṁ na pariyādāya tiṭṭhati, nindāpi cittaṁ na pariyādāya tiṭṭhati, pasaṁsāpi cittaṁ na pariyādāya tiṭṭhati, sukhampi cittaṁ na pariyādāya tiṭṭhati, dukkhampi cittaṁ na pariyādāya tiṭṭhati.
root_text.an8.6:6.2: So uppannaṁ lābhaṁ nānurujjhati, alābhe nappaṭivirujjhati;
root_text.an8.6:6.6: So evaṁ anurodhavirodhavippahīno parimuccati jātiyā jarāya maraṇena sokehi paridevehi dukkhehi domanassehi upāyāsehi.
root_text.an8.6:6.8: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, viseso ayaṁ adhippayāso idaṁ nānākaraṇaṁ sutavato ariyasāvakassa assutavatā puthujjanenāti.

translation_text.an8.6:1.1: “Mendicants, the eight worldly conditions revolve around the world, and the world revolves around the eight worldly conditions.
translation_text.an8.6:1.3: Gain and loss, fame and disgrace, blame and praise, pleasure and pain.
translation_text.an8.6:1.4: These eight worldly conditions revolve around the world, and the world revolves around these eight worldly conditions.
translation_text.an8.6:2.1: An unlearned ordinary person encounters gain and loss, fame and disgrace, blame and praise, and pleasure and pain.
translation_text.an8.6:2.2: And so does a learned noble disciple.
translation_text.an8.6:2.3: What, then, is the difference between a learned noble disciple and an ordinary unlearned person?”
translation_text.an8.6:2.4: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. He is our guide and our refuge. Sir, may the Buddha himself please clarify the meaning of this. The mendicants will listen and remember it.”
translation_text.an8.6:3.1: “Well then, mendicants, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an8.6:3.4: “Mendicants, an unlearned ordinary person encounters gain.
translation_text.an8.6:3.7: It’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable.’ They don’t truly understand it.
translation_text.an8.6:3.17: It’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable.’ They don’t truly understand it.
translation_text.an8.6:4.1: So gain and loss, fame and disgrace, blame and praise, and pleasure and pain occupy their mind.
translation_text.an8.6:4.2: They favor gain and oppose loss.
translation_text.an8.6:4.3: They favor fame and oppose disgrace.
translation_text.an8.6:4.4: They favor praise and oppose blame.
translation_text.an8.6:4.5: They favor pleasure and oppose pain.
translation_text.an8.6:4.6: Being so full of favoring and opposing, they’re not freed from rebirth, old age, and death, from sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress.
translation_text.an8.6:4.7: They’re not freed from suffering, I say.
translation_text.an8.6:5.1: A learned noble disciple encounters gain.
translation_text.an8.6:5.4: It’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable.’ They truly understand it.
translation_text.an8.6:5.14: It’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable.’ They truly understand it.
translation_text.an8.6:6.1: So gain and loss, fame and disgrace, blame and praise, and pleasure and pain don’t occupy their mind.
translation_text.an8.6:6.2: They don’t favor gain or oppose loss.
translation_text.an8.6:6.3: They don’t favor fame or oppose disgrace.
translation_text.an8.6:6.4: They don’t favor praise or oppose blame.
translation_text.an8.6:6.5: They don’t favor pleasure or oppose pain.
translation_text.an8.6:6.6: Having given up favoring and opposing, they’re freed from rebirth, old age, and death, from sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress.
translation_text.an8.6:6.7: They’re freed from suffering, I say.
translation_text.an8.6:6.8: This is the difference between a learned noble disciple and an unlearned ordinary person.
translation_text.an8.6:7.1: Gain and loss, fame and disgrace,
translation_text.an8.6:7.2: blame and praise, and pleasure and pain.
translation_text.an8.6:7.3: These qualities among mankind are impermanent,
translation_text.an8.6:8.1: An intelligent and mindful person knows these things,
translation_text.an8.6:8.3: Desirable things don’t disturb their mind,
translation_text.an8.6:8.4: nor are they repelled by the undesirable.
translation_text.an8.6:9.2: are cleared and disappeared, they are no more.
translation_text.an8.6:9.4: they who have gone beyond rebirth <j>understand rightly.”
